stories.title: France’s far-right leader Jordan Bardella tours Poland in search for new allies
stories.summary: WARSAW — Jordan Bardella is shopping for allies.  As he prepares for a potential run for the French presidency next year, the leader of the far-right National Rally used a trip to Poland this week to court nationalist and conservative partners to join him in trying to shape the EU to his liking.  “This visit is a key step in a series of European trips,” Bardella said during a press conference at the Polish parliament Thursday. He said he had had a “very fruitful” meeting with Polish Pr

stories.title: Costa outreach to Russia was ‘misguided,’ Estonian PM says
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Efforts by European Council President António Costa to open diplomatic backchannels to the Kremlin could undermine the bloc’s support for Ukraine, Estonia’s prime minister has warned in the most staunch condemnation of the move yet. In comments to POLITICO following the first day of a summit of leaders in Brussels, Kristen Michal said Russia continues to reject any realistic path to peace — and insisted any diplomacy must be led by Kyiv. “The European Union cannot as

stories.title: Macron, Merz attack EU’s stance on Putin talks
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Emmanuel Macron and Friedrich Merz struck out against the EU for opening up communication with Vladimir Putin, putting the leaders of Europe’s two largest countries on collision course with a large part of the rest of the bloc. In an unexpectedly discordant late-night summit in Brussels ― the first since 2010 without longtime contrarian ex-Hungary Prime Minister Viktor Orbán ― the French president and the German chancellor scorned efforts by European Council President António Co

stories.title: The EU is for the birds
stories.summary: Welcome to Declassified, a weekly humor column . The name for a group of owls is a parliament. Presumably because, like politicians, they have deep-set eyes, toes that point in different directions and swallow their prey whole. Alas, there is no collective noun for politicians (A scandal of politicians? A promise of politicians? A drone of politicians?). But if anyone decided to release some owls into a legislative chamber, it would be a parliament in a parliament, making headline writers the wo

stories.title: Some EU leaders furious at Costa official’s calls with the Kremlin
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU’s communication with the Kremlin has provoked a furious backlash among some European governments, which complained they weren’t informed. European Council President António Costa’s chief of staff, Pedro Lourtie, twice over the past few weeks contacted officials in Moscow as the EU looked for ways to restart negotiations with Russian President Vladimir Putin. With U.S.-led attempts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine appearing deadlocked, European capital

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Gas prices fall below $4 on average after Trump’s signing of Iran deal to end war
stories.summary: Drivers feel some relief but prices still a dollar more per gallon overall since before US-Israel attack on Iran Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The average price of US gasoline fell to just under $4 a gallon on Thursday for the first time since March , following the announcement of a preliminary agreement between the US and Iran to end the war and reopen the strait of Hormuz. The development has provided some relief to drivers who have seen soaring costs amid Washington’s war

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: A Fidesz-kampányt szervező alapítványtól igazolt új kurátort a Transzparens Újságírásért Alapítvány. Aztán úgy tűnt, hogy megszűnt
stories.summary: Mondhatnánk, hogy az új igazolással megkérdőjeleződött az újságírás átláthatóságáért küzdő alapítvány függetlensége, de nem szeretnénk ilyesmivel nevetségessé tenni magunkat. Közben az alapítvány olyan transzparens lett, hogy teljesen el is tűnt: a honlapja elérhetetlenné vált, és az emailcíme is megszűnt. Előbbi viszont azóta vissza is tért.

stories.title: Csehország–Dél-afrikai Köztársaság 1-1
Molnár Zsolt kilép az MSZP-ből
stories.summary: Az óbudai korrupciós ügy egyik kulcsfigurája azzal indokolta a döntését, hogy nem szeretné, hogy az ügy személyes vagy politikai terhet jelentsen a Magyar Szocialista Párt számára.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nem belpolitikai, pártpolitikai okokból fogunk akár egyetérteni, akár vétózni
stories.summary: Az EU-csúcs előtt a V4-es tárgyalásáról, a brüsszeli fogadtatásáról és a napi egymillió eurós büntetésről is beszélt a kormányfő.

stories.title: A V4-ek vezetőivel posztolt képet Magyar Péter, és azt írta, újraindítják a Visegrádi Együttműködést
stories.summary: Azt is megerősítette, hogy június 23-án érkezik Budapestre és Gödöllőre a lengyel, a cseh és a szlovák miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Brüsszelben: A határidőt nem rajtunk kellene számonkérni, mi most kármentésben vagyunk
stories.summary: A Magyarországot sújtó napi egymillió eurós bírságról azt mondta, hogy dolgoznak a megoldáson, de nem tudta megmondani, mikorra mentesülhet az ország a menekültügyi szabályozás miatt kiszabott büntetés alól.

stories.title: Osztrák kancellár az első EU-csúcson Magyar Péterrel: Magyarország egy Európa-párti utat választott
stories.summary: Andrej Plenković horvát miniszterelnök uniós ügyekben konstruktív megközelítésre, a kétoldalú kapcsolatokban új szakaszra számít Magyar Pétertől.

stories.title: Az erőtlen csehek Dél-Afrikával sem bírtak, 1-1-re végeztek
stories.summary: Ezzel a döntetlennel jó eséllyel mindkét csapat búcsúzik a vb-től.

stories.title: Magyar: "We will represent Hungary differently"
stories.summary: Ahead of his first EUCO summit, the Hungarian PM spoke about how he intends to represent Hungary at the EU, how he was welcomed in Brussels, and the upcoming meeting of the V4 in Hungary.

stories.title: Októberig kapott haladékot Budapest a szolidaritási hozzájárulásra, de akkor 86 milliárdot kell majd fizetnie
stories.summary: A Fővárosi Önkormányzat szerint a kormány megértette, hogy egy újabb inkasszó bármely időpontban fizetésképtelenségbe sodorhatná Budapestet.

stories.title: Lássuk meg, mit lehet tenni – mondta az EP-elnök az uniós ezermilliárdokat érintő perről
stories.summary: A képviselőkkel egyeztetve fognak lépni az igazságügyi csomag miatti perben és a hetes cikkes eljárásban is, közölte a Telex kérdésére Roberta Metsola, miután Magyar Péterrel tárgyalt.

stories.title: 444: Költségvetési csalás gyanúja miatt nyomoznak a MOL – Új Európa Alapítvánnyal szemben
stories.summary: Felmerült, hogy az alapítvány szabálytalan szerződéseken keresztül sokszor százmillió forintnyi adót nem fizetett meg a magyar államnak.

stories.title: Irán megtanulta a leckét, de nem azt, amire Trump várt
stories.summary: A G7-országok vezetői üdvözölték a Trump és az iráni elnök által szerda este aláírt megállapodást, ami újranyitotta a kulcsfontosságú Hormuzi-szorost. A demokraták mellett viszont a republikánus politikusok sem ugrálnak örömükben. Van, aki szerint ez az elmúlt évtizedek legrosszabb külpolitikai fiaskója.

stories.title: Több mint egy évig nem sikerült, újra közösen adták ki a következtetéseket az EU-csúcson Ukrajnáról
stories.summary: Orbán Viktor tavaly márciusban bontotta meg az egységet, később még egy vezetőt magával tudott rántani. Magyar Péter szerint a kezdeményezésére az utolsó pillanatban került ki egy rész a szövegből.

stories.title: Sok élelmiszer lett olcsóbb a boltokban, de ennek a magyar termelők ihatják meg a levét
stories.summary: Az árrésstop és az erős forint kombinációja az importtermékek felé tereli a boltokat, a magyar agrárium és élelmiszeripar kárára.

stories.title: Lázár János és Szijjártó Péter alig jár be a parlamentbe, de eddig megúszták a büntetést
stories.summary: A két fideszes képviselő állandó távolmaradását Magyar Péter osztályfőnök módjára többször is szóvá tette. A házszabály szerint a házelnök csökkentheti is az igazolatlanul hiányzó képviselők fizetését. Kocsis Máté elküldte, hogy ő mivel indokolta az eddigi távolmaradásait.

stories.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms
stories.summary: Exclusive: health professionals, survivors and politicians voice concerns in open letter over comments by Fatima Maada Bio, who denies supporting the practice The first lady of Sierra Leone has denied that she supports female genital mutilation amid rising anger around her perceived approval of the practice. But in an exclusive response to the Guardian, Fatima Maada Bio, the wife of President Julius Maada Bio, also said she would not openly condemn FGM until she saw “reliable data” that the prac

stories.title: A politikailag jól ismert szereplőket szűrné ki a kormány a dohány- és kaszinókoncessziókból
stories.summary: A Magyarországon működő közel 5800 dohánybolt ellátásáért egy nagy hódmezővásárhelyi cég felel. Tulajdonosai a cég alapításának első 10 évében 52,5 milliárd forintot vettek fel osztalékként.

stories.title: Kovács István játékvezető különleges mezben mutatkozik be, övé az ezredik vb-meccs
stories.summary: A FIFA-bírófőnök, Pierluigi Collina a romániai játékvezetőnek adta a megtiszteltetést, hogy a történelem része legyen.

stories.title: Kifulladt a növekedés, csúsznak le a magyar webshopok, a Temu tarol az e-kereskedelemben
stories.summary: Sokkal gyorsabban nő a Temu, mint a piacméret, vagyis a versenytársak elől viszi el a magyar vásárlókat.

stories.title: Málna alig, fekete ribizli talán egyáltalán nem terem a jövőben Magyarországon
stories.summary: A magyar málna-, ribizli- és szedertermelés is óriási kihívásokkal küzd, a nyolcvanas évekhez képest 90 százalékkal kevesebb málnásunk van. A vegyszerezett import pedig kevesebb ízt és tápanyagot tartalmaz.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely kéri vissza a Városligetet Budapestnek
stories.summary: A főpolgármester arra reagált, hogy a kormány kimondta: nem épül új épület a parkban.

stories.title: The Guardian view on defending Europe in a new era: collaboration is the key | Editorial
stories.summary: The recent abandonment of plans for a Franco-German fighter jet sent a disastrous signal. Strategic autonomy will be jointly achieved or not at all It has become a truism to assert that Europe needs to fast-track its own strategic independence in a volatile world. A recent paper from the European Council on Foreign Relations describes the continent’s leaders as grappling with “a ‘Schrödinger’s NATO’ moment, in which America remains formally inside the alliance while behaving as though it were no

stories.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
stories.summary: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
stories.summary: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

stories.title: What did Ukraine target in Moscow and how significant was the drone attack?
stories.summary: Mass drone strike on capital brought the war to Russians, but Ukrainians will be braced for Kremlin’s response Ukraine hit Moscow with nearly 200 drones in its largest ever attack on the Russian capital on Thursday, striking an oil refinery and sending huge plumes of smoke billowing over the city’s south. The towering columns of smoke rising above Moscow offered a stark demonstration of Ukraine’s growing ability to strike deep inside Russia with its increasingly sophisticated, largely domestical

stories.title: Middle East crisis: Trump says US expects ‘complete ceasefire on all fronts, including Lebanon, Hezbollah and Israel’ – as it happened
stories.summary: US president says he encourages Middle East countries to ‘maintain commitment to allowing our negotiations to beautifully unfold’ Reaction: Donald Trump’s Iran deal met with anger, relief and incredulity Analysis: Trump’s Iran deal is result of unrealistic ambitions for an untenable war Donald Trump had urged Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu to “stop blowing up buildings” during a phone call about Israel’s military campaign in Lebanon, according to a report in the Wall Street Journal. T

stories.title: US military kills three people in boat strike in Pacific Ocean
stories.summary: Attack brings to at least 211 number of people killed as Trump administration targets alleged ‘narcoterrorists’ The US military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Thursday, killing three people, as the Trump administration wages a months-long campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America. The latest attack brings the number of people the US military has killed in boat strikes to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting people it

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war
stories.summary: Kyiv says attack, which also forced evacuation at Russia’s biggest airport, was in response to strike on historic monastery Explainer: how significant was attack on Moscow? Ukrainian drones have hit several locations across Moscow in Kyiv’s biggest air raid on the city since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion, setting a major ⁠oil refinery on fire and forcing evacuations at the country’s largest airport. Volodymyr Zelenskyy described the attack as a response to Russia’s strike on a histor

stories.title: Tories beat SNP in Aberdeen South after relentless North Sea oil campaign
stories.summary: The Conservatives roundly beat the Scottish National Party in the Aberdeen South by-election Friday morning — after weeks of hammering the ruling Scottish Party over restrictions on oil and gas drilling. The Tories overturned a more than 3,000-vote majority in the Scottish seat, a thumping Conservative win — and their first by-election gain north of the border since the late 1960s. Douglas Lumsden, a long-serving member of the Scottish Parliament, will now make the leap to Westminster, after a c

stories.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the traffick

stories.title: Pete Hegseth accuses Nato countries of ‘free riding’ in combative address
stories.summary: US defence secretary addresses allies in latest attempt to get Europe to raise military budgets Pete Hegseth has announced a review of the US military presence across Europe, in a combative address to Nato allies where he threatened to cut force numbers in countries spending the least on defence. The US defence secretary, speaking at a meeting of Nato defence ministers in Brussels, accused some countries of “free riding” and others of being shameful for not allowing their airbases to be used by

stories.title: Mangione lawyers abandon psychiatric defense over health CEO’s killing
stories.summary: Legal team of alleged gunman, 28, reverses position on ‘extreme emotional disturbance’ defense after one day In a stunning reversal, Luigi Mangione’s legal team said on Thursday they would no longer pursue a psychiatric defense in his upcoming state trial over the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. Just one day earlier , Mangione’s lawyers told Judge Gregory Carro they would pursue a defense claiming the 28-year-old was suffering an “extreme emotional disturbance” at the time of

stories.title: Man arrested after boy, three, injured in Cambridgeshire zoo crocodile enclosure
stories.summary: Officers arrest man on suspicion of attempted murder as child is treated in hospital for serious injuries A man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a three-year-old boy ended up in a crocodile enclosure, Cambridgeshire police said. The force said officers were called to Johnsons of Old Hurst zoo in Huntingdonshire at 1.24pm on Thursday over “reports of an incident involving a three-year-old boy, during which he ended up in the crocodile enclosure”. Continue reading...

stories.title: JD Vance tells Iran deal critics in Israel: Trump is your only ally left in the world
stories.summary: In a sharp rebuke to critics of Trump’s deal with Iran, the vice-president referenced the billions in defence aid Israel recieves from the US News: Iran announces plans to bring in maritime fees for strait of Hormuz Analysis: Iran peace deal makes clear how far US has been forced to retreat since 2025 US vice-president JD Vance has lashed out at Israeli critics of the Iran deal, saying Donald Trump is Israel’s only ally left in the world, in a sharp rebuke that referenced the billions in defence

stories.title: California ‘billionaire tax’ makes ballot despite opposition from tech moguls
stories.summary: Wealth tax criticized by billionaires and Gavin Newsom would levy a one-time 5% tax on residents worth over $1bn A popular proposal in California to impose a wealth tax on billionaires has gained enough signatures to qualify for the ballot in November, state officials announced on Wednesday. The news is set to intensify an already heated debate around the tax, which has pitted tech moguls and the state’s governor, Gavin Newsom, against the labor union backing the measure. Continue reading...

stories.title: Scottish Conservatives win Aberdeen South, ousting SNP
stories.summary: Tories’ Douglas Lumsden says city has spoken ‘loud and clear’ in support of the North Sea oil and gas industry UK politics – live updates The Scottish National party (SNP) has lost the formerly safe seat of Aberdeen South in a shock loss to the Scottish Conservatives. The Scottish Conservatives’ Douglas Lumsden beat the SNP’s Richard Thomson by 6,050 votes, with a 14.69% swing away from the SNP. The Tories vote share was 49.51%. Lumsden’s vote tally was 14,308, with Thomson on 8,258. Continue re

stories.title: Makerfield byelection: Andy Burnham says Labour has ‘final chance to change’ after huge win over Reform UK – UK politics live
stories.summary: Burnham hails a ‘turning point’ for the country after a resounding victory over Reform UK and Restore that may force prime minister Keir Starmer to step aside Full report: Andy Burnham wins huge majority in Makerfield byelection, paving way for Starmer leadership challenge Burnham brings in top economists before possible leadership run David Blunkett , the former Labour cabinet minister, has suggested that Keir Starmer should stand down after the Makerfield byelection. In an interview on the BBC

stories.title: Australia news live: Tony Modra still in critical condition as wife thanks first responders after truck accident
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live • Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast There were two small earthquakes in New South Wales this morning, according to Geoscience Australia. A 3.4 magnitude quake hit near Orange around 5.45am this morning, with at least 108 people reporting they felt the shakes. And a 3.2 magnitude temblor hit near Wilcannia two hours earlier. Just two people reported feeling that quake. If we’re given the choice between taking the easier path politically or doing

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit oil refinery and residential building in Moscow – video
stories.summary: Flames and plumes of smoke filled the sky after a Moscow oil refinery was stuck in a Ukrainian drone attack. Up to 180 drones that were purportedly headed towards the Russian capital were shot down by air defence forces, according to Moscow's mayor. A high-rise residential building near the refinery was also damaged in the attack Moscow oil refinery on fire after ‘large-scale’ Ukrainian drone strike Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.title: Anger at ‘send them back’ chants by rightwing MEPs after EU migration law vote
stories.summary: Other lawmakers respond with ‘shame on you’ in heated confrontation over passing of plan to increase deportations Rightwing MEPs have come under fire after they celebrated a vote aimed at increasing deportations across the EU with chants of “send them back”, leading other lawmakers to respond with cries of “shame on you”. The heated confrontation in the European parliament came on Wednesday after lawmakers voted 418 to 218 to approve controversial measures aimed at increasing deportations of und

stories.title: ‘If Ukraine burns, your Moscow will burn,’ Zelenskyy warns after overnight strikes in Russia – as it happened
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president ramps up rhetoric after overnight drone strikes on Russian capital Hegseth makes it clear that the review will not be just a box-ticking exercise. “ It’s a review that some countries will fail and others will pass with flying colours. In the end, the review is intended to both improve US force posture and basing and strengthen Nato 3.0.” “It will be designed to ensure that Nato is moving fast and irreversibly toward Europe leading , stepping up to take primary responsibility

stories.title: EU summit: Leaders discuss Ukraine, global issues
stories.summary: European Union leaders agreed to extend sanctions against Russia for 12 months. Meeting in Brussels, the leaders are also set to address the bloc's next long-term budget and global economic challenges.

stories.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
stories.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

stories.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
stories.summary: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

stories.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
stories.summary: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

stories.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
stories.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

stories.title: EU leaders demand von der Leyen tools up against China
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU leaders asked the European Commission to come up with tougher tools to deal with China’s economic might, with Germany warming to a harsher position toward Beijing. Over late-night dinner at a summit in Brussels, leaders requested the Commission assess the EU’s trade defense tool box and “eventually” come up with new instruments, to ensure the bloc has what it “needs to defend its interests and derisk,” according to an EU official. Leaders want the Commission to keep engaging with B

stories.title: Andy Burnham decisively wins Makerfield by-election
stories.summary: LONDON — Andy Burnham cleared a major hurdle in his quest to become Britain’s next prime minister Friday — winning the Makerfield by-election. Labour’s Burnham comfortably beat his nearest rival, Reform UK’s Robert Kenyon, by more than 9,000 votes in the special contest. It’s a win which will significantly boost his standing among fellow Labour MPs who are already considering ditching Keir Starmer, a prime minister who has struggled to connect with the British public. Burnham wi

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Cynical to get power’: Michel Barnier on Boris Johnson, Brexit and the EU’s future
stories.summary: Former negotiator believes in an unstable world, it is ‘perfectly possible’ the UK can rejoin the EU with old opt-outs UK could keep special pre-Brexit terms if it rejoined EU, Michel Barnier says A couple of years ago, Michel Barnier spent a weekend with Boris Johnson’s father, Stanley. It was not some ghoulish Brexit spin-off of The Traitors, but the result of the former EU negotiator’s wife, Isabelle, being a close friend of Johnson’s French cousin, Anne du Boucheron, the owner of Château de

stories.title: Polls close in Makerfield byelection as Andy Burnham eyes No 10
stories.summary: Labour’s Greater Manchester mayor predicted to beat Reform but Keir Starmer is unlikely to easily step aside Makerfield byelection – latest updates Polls have closed in Makerfield, Aberdeen South, and Arbroath and Broughty Ferry, with the result in the north-west of England constituency byelection poised to decide the future of UK politics. The population of Makerfield , a constituency sitting just outside Wigan, has found itself at the centre of the British political world in recent weeks after

stories.title: Iran announces plans to bring in maritime fees for strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: As US lifts its blockade, Tehran says fees to cover cost of managing waterway will come into effect at end of 60-day negotiation period Iran has announced plans to introduce a system of maritime fees in the strait of Hormuz in two months, after the 60-day period of negotiation that has been triggered by the signing of the memorandum of understanding. Tehran, claiming a historic victory over the US, said the strait was under its control and a European plan for a naval mission to escort ships thou

stories.title: Lawsuit from Democratic-led ⁠states can proceed over Trump’s order to restrict mail-in voting – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Sign up for the Breaking News US email He calls the situation a “win-win” for the US. Vance is here, and he starts by claiming that Trump’s peace deal with Iran “is already bearing real fruits for the American people”, with 12.5m barrels going through the strait of Hormuz last night and gas prices dropping below $4 today for the first time since the conflict began. Continue reading...

stories.title: Andy Burnham wins huge majority in Makerfield byelection, paving way for Starmer leadership challenge
stories.summary: Greater Manchester mayor says result represents Labour’s ‘final chance to change’, after soundly beating Reform UK and hardline Restore Britain UK politics – live updates Andy Burnham has won the crucial Makerfield byelection by a huge majority, paving the way for a challenge to Keir Starmer’s premiership. The Greater Manchester mayor beat the Reform UK candidate, Robert Kenyon, by 9,231 votes, and the new hardline Restore Britain party coming a distant third.. Continue reading...

stories.title: Rejoining customs union would not fix damage caused by Brexit, research finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Economists find Brexit caused 12% depression in UK exports, most of which is due to leaving single market Brexit has depressed UK exports to the EU by 12%, and rejoining the customs union would undo only a fraction of the damage, research shared with the Guardian shows. With the UK’s future relationship with the bloc likely to feature prominently in a potential Labour leadership contest, the economists John Springford and Anton Spisak, of the Centre for European Reform, provide fresh

stories.title: Norway’s monarchy once seemed like a fairytale – recent crises have exposed its dark underbelly | Magnus Nome
stories.summary: The royals were always popular, but Crown Princess Mette-Marit’s Epstein connections and her son’s rape convictions have profoundly shaken public trust The Norwegian monarchy is in crisis. Not because its future queen is gravely ill , nor even because her son has this week been convicted of serious crimes , but because the institution’s greatest asset – public trust – has been eroded by a series of self-inflicted mistakes. Yesterday, it was announced that Norway’s crown princess, Mette-Marit, un

stories.title: Australian net overseas migration falls to lowest level since 2022 – but the Coalition says that’s still too high
stories.summary: Australian Bureau of Statistics figures show migration growth is above pre-pandemic levels but tracking steadily down Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Net overseas migration added 301,000 people to Australia’s population last year, the lowest increase since mid-2022 but still above the pre-pandemic pace. stories.summary: David Farley was told One Nation’s policy was to fly only the Australian flag, party leader tells conference Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation’s leader, Pauline Hanson, says she “had to have a conversation” with the new Farrer MP, David Farley, after he appeared to defy her policies on immigration and flying Indigenous flags. stories.title: UAE imposes limits on teen social media access
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has announced a social media ban for all children under 15. Platforms have one year to review and remove under-age accounts or face stiff penalties.

stories.title: US probes Germany's 'persistent underpayment' for drugs
stories.summary: The US Trade Representative said a new probe would determine if Germany was a underpaying for pharmaceuticals.

stories.title: Germany news: Minister weighs cuts to UN contributions
stories.summary: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has criticized the UN after Germany failed in a recent bid for a Security Council seat. Meanwhile, industrial employment levels in the country are at a decade low.

stories.title: NATO: US lashes out, alliance head says everything's fine
stories.summary: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth threatened NATO allies over lack of spending and tepid support for the war in Iran. Members say they are on the right path but need time as NATO boss Mark Rutte, claims all is good.

stories.title: Oil nations on edge in the face of new climate coalition
stories.summary: Even amid an ongoing energy crunch, major economies are dodging firm timelines to phase out coal, oil and gas. Fossil fuel-producing nations now fear new clean energy alliances could ramp up the pressure to go green.

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
stories.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

stories.title: Lebanon: Tyre residents return to homes shattered by strikes
stories.summary: Lebanese families returning to Tyre found their homes in ruins on 18 June after Israeli strikes and voiced cautious optimism about a new US-Iran agreement.

stories.title: Parents of Serbia's teenage school shooter given jail terms in retrial
stories.summary: The boy was 13 when he killed eight girls, a boy and a school guard at Vladislav Ribnikar school in Belgrade in 2023.

stories.title: Iran's ayatollah says he approved US framework deal despite having 'different view'
stories.summary: Meanwhile, US Vice President JD Vance defended the deal, saying he would likely head to Switzerland for talks to turn it into a long-term agreement.

stories.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
stories.summary: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

stories.title: Knicks revel in NBA victory parade as thousands of fans fill New York's streets
stories.summary: New York erupted in jubilant scenes on Saturday when the Knicks broke a 53-year drought to defeat the San Antonio Spurs away in Texas.

stories.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

stories.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
stories.summary: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.

stories.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
stories.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.

stories.title: Obamas host star-studded opening of Chicago presidential centre
stories.summary: George W Bush, Bill Clinton and Joe Biden attended, but Donald Trump was not invited.

stories.title: US lifts naval blockade as Iran's supreme leader says Trump made deal 'out of desperation'
stories.summary: Iran's supreme leader says he disagrees with the deal and Donald Trump signed it "out of desperation".

stories.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
stories.summary: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

stories.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
stories.summary: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

stories.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
stories.summary: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.

stories.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
stories.summary: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

stories.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
stories.summary: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

stories.title: Makerfield by-election: Burnham beats Reform, aims at Starmer — live updates
stories.summary: Andy Burnham cleared a major hurdle in his quest to become Britain’s next prime minister — decisively winning the Makerfield by-election. Burnham comfortably beat his nearest rival, Reform UK’s Robert Kenyon, by more than 9,000 votes, a win which will significantly boost his standing among fellow Labour MPs who are already considering ditching Keir Starmer, a prime minister who has struggled to connect with the British public.  Burnham will now have a seat in parliament from which he can di

stories.title: Burnham says he wants to ensure Makerfield byelection ‘changes British politics’, in eve-of-poll speech – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog has now closed. See all our UK politics coverage here Andy Burnham may have trouble getting through to Keir Starmer if he tries ringing him after the Makerfield byelection to urge him to set a timetable for his departure. Burnham reportedly wants to call Starmer this weekend. (See 9.47am .) stories.title: Is Russia heading for a fuel crisis?
stories.summary: Russia's fuel output is falling after Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries. Seasonal spikes in gasoline and diesel demand could push the system into a deeper crisis, experts say.

stories.title: Putin meets with ASEAN leaders in Russia as G7 vow 'unwavering' support for Ukraine
stories.summary: It comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with G7 leaders in France. Zelenskyy said on Wednesday they had agreed on the "additional strengthening of Ukraine’s air defense" and new measures against Moscow.

stories.title: Cuba approves economic reforms amid US pressure
stories.summary: Cuba's Communist Party has signed off on a package of free market reforms. One member called the changes a response to "economic war," while the party said they were not a "deviation" from the socialist project.

stories.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
stories.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

stories.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
stories.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

stories.title: Niger security forces battle gunmen at airport
stories.summary: Niger's security forces engaged in an hours-long shootout with gunmen at the nation's main international airport. No one has taken responsibility but Islamists are suspected of being behind the attack.

stories.title: US-Iran deal: Between conflict and compromise
stories.summary: The US and Iran are starting tough negotiations after signing a ceasefire agreement. DW looks at the red lines, and where both sides could agree.

stories.title: India news: Telegram is 'like Frankenstein,' government says
stories.summary: The platform — temporarily banned in India — has grown into a "monster," officials say. Elswhere, the air force is helping secure the NEET-UG retests, while Modi and Trump are "very close" to trade deal. DW has more.

stories.title: US-Iran talks: Hope and skepticism in the Swiss alps
stories.summary: World leaders welcomed an end to fighting and relief from a global energy crunch — but critics doubt a lasting deal is in sight. Will history be made in Switzerland?

stories.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

stories.title: World Cup: Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi denied entry to Canada
stories.summary: Elephants striker Elye Wahi is being investigated in France in connection to allegations of match-fixing. The 23-year-old, who is contracted to Eintracht Frankfurt, is not the first player to be denied a Canadian visa.

stories.title: Trump signs initial deal to end Iran war and open Strait of Hormuz as nuclear talks continue
stories.summary: Trump signed an initial framework deal to restore the pre-war status quo between Iran and the United States, inking an initial agreement towards ending a war he started with Israel on 28 February.

stories.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran deal at Palace of Versailles
stories.summary: Video released by the French presidency shows Donald Trump signing an agreement aimed at ending the war with Iran during a dinner at Versailles, with French President Emmanuel Macron seated beside him.

stories.title: UK: Tommy Robinson’s Oxford Union debate sparks protests and free speech backlash
stories.summary: Tommy Robinson’s appearance at the Oxford Union prompts large protests and reignites the UK debate over free expression and platforms for extremists.

stories.title: Watch: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Versailles
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran at the Palace of Versailles on 18 June, marking a significant diplomatic step aimed at easing the Middle East conflict that has unsettled the region since February.

stories.title: Lil Nas X says he's 'feeling better' after rehab and bipolar diagnosis
stories.summary: The Old Town Road star was arrested last year for attacking police officers and later entered rehab.

stories.title: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
stories.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.

stories.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

stories.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
stories.summary: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

stories.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
stories.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

stories.title: British man dies in paragliding accident in Spain

stories.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
stories.summary: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

stories.title: First Russian shadow fleet tanker enters Channel since Smyrtos boarding
stories.summary: Forwarder, a Russian-flagged ship which left port in Primorsk last week, entered the Channel on Wednesday evening.

stories.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
stories.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

stories.summary: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

stories.title: Jury fails to reach verdict in Norwegian 'hitman' trial
stories.summary: Johannes Natland, 19, was remanded in custody and will now face a retrial.

stories.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
stories.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

stories.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
stories.summary: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

stories.title: Ezek a filmek elérték, hogy eszedbe se jusson a mobilod a moziban
stories.summary: Előbb jött az új Barbenheimer, mint gondoltuk volna. A mozikban két olyan film a fő beszédtéma, amelyeket huszonéves, YouTube-on edződött alkotók készítettek kis költségvetésből. A Backrooms és a Megszállottság receptjét akarja mindenki megfejteni.

stories.summary: Egy friss kanadai jelentés szerint a hajót nem tesztelték eleget, az olyan munkatársakat pedig, akik biztonsági aggályokat vetettek fel, kirúgták a cégtől. A tengeralattjáróban végül öten vesztették életüket.

stories.summary: Az amerikai alelnök szerint nem támasztják alá tények azt a felvetést, hogy rossz megállapodást kötöttek Iránnal.

stories.summary: Az Arsenal nagyban ennek a taktikai húzásnak köszönheti, hogy 22 év után bajnoki címet nyert, de a világbajnokságon is főszerephez juthat a kapusokat ellehetetlenítő játékelem. Norvégia már profitált belőle, de az igazi dara a csoportkör után jöhet.

stories.summary: Pelczné Gáll Ildikó szerint „az uniós források eredményes védelme érdekében az Európai Bizottságnak ambiciózusabb megközelítéssel nagyobb hangsúlyt kell fektetnie az eredményekre”.

stories.summary: Többen is arról számoltak be, hogy szitáló eső hullott, amitől a ruhájukon fekete foltok jelentek meg. Az orosz hatóságok tagadják, hogy olajos esőt okozott volna az olajfinomítóban történt robbanás.

stories.summary: Egy friss felmérés szerint a hazai exportcégek sokasága vált veszteségessé az elmúlt két hónap 10 százalékos forinterősödése miatt. Egyik szövetségük segélykiáltásnak is beillő eredményeket tett közzé a kamatdöntés előtt.

stories.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
stories.summary: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Protesters to rally against World Cup sponsor Hyundai before Mexico game
stories.summary: Focus on business dealings with mining company Guadalajara rally to highlight fate of ‘disappeared’ Hyundai will be targeted by protesters at a rally before the Group A game between Mexico and South Korea in Guadalajara on Thursday, due to the World Cup sponsor’s business dealings with the South American mining company Ternium. A 2025 report from the environmental group Mighty Earth criticised Hyundai’s involvement in what they described as a “dirty steel supply chain”, as the South Korean motor

stories.title: Starmer warns against ‘looking backwards’ to Brexit after rivals back UK’s return to EU
stories.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, prime minister says he stands by Labour’s manifesto pledge not to rejoin bloc The UK and the European Union should not waste time “looking backwards” to Brexit, Keir Starmer said on Wednesday, as he comes under pressure to reconsider rejoining the EU. The prime minister reaffirmed his government’s manifesto commitment to not re-enter the bloc, but said there had been “real progress” with the relationship, which was “slowly but surely building”. stories.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
stories.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

stories.title: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
stories.summary: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

stories.title: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

stories.title: Luigi Mangione to use psychiatric defence in health CEO murder trial, judge says
stories.summary: Brian Thompson was killed in December 2024 as he walked to a Manhattan hotel for UnitedHealth Group’s annual investor conference.

stories.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
stories.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

stories.title: Labour came to power with no big idea for relations with EU, says former top diplomat
stories.summary: Ivan Rogers, Britain’s EU ambassador from 2013 to 2017, says party’s ideas did not ‘remotely measure up’ to challenge Labour arrived in power with no big idea on the UK’s future relationship with the EU, a former British ambassador to Brussels has said. stories.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue read

stories.title: Abandoned pony grows his strength in fourth floor flat – video
stories.summary: When Mile the pony was rejected by his mother at birth, his owner, Andjelka Josipovic, decided to take him in. Each night she carries him up to her fourth-floor flat in the Croatian town of Rovinj. Mile spends his days on a ranch but needs regular feeding and attention during the night. Josipovic hopes that after a few weeks he will be strong enough to stay on the ranch full time Continue reading...

stories.title: Why did Russian warship fire warning shots in the Channel? – The Latest
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has called the firing of warning shots by a Russian warship at a British yacht sailing across the Channel on Tuesday ‘deeply concerning and reckless’. Russia’s defence ministry said the yacht was on a ‘dangerous course’ and several attempts were made to contact it – a claim disputed by the retired couple onboard the 40ft yacht. Nosheen Iqbal speaks to the Guardian’s defence and security editor, Dan Sabbagh Continue reading...

stories.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds

The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the A

stories.title: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds
stories.summary: Review reveals rise in users and rates of psychosis in countries where cannabis is sold commercially Decriminalising the possession of cannabis or strictly regulating access to the drug do not appear to drive up usage, but when the drug is sold commercially the number of users increases and more mental health problems are seen, a review has found. An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug,

stories.title: Pósfai Gábor leváltotta a Nemzeti Nyomozó Iroda és a Nemzeti Védelmi Szolgálat vezetőjét is
stories.summary: Jeney Áron dandártábornok helyére Schőn Péter Ernő ezredes kerül.

stories.title: Vádat emeltek a mezőcsáti polgármester nyelvvizsgapótlékos ügyében
stories.summary: Siposné Horváth Anita úgy vehetett fel nyelvi pótlékot, hogy nem volt erre feljogosító nyelvvizsgája.

stories.title: Első fokon hat és fél év fegyházra ítélték Joób Márton világbajnok kenust, de szabadlábon maradhat a jogerős döntésig
stories.summary: A bíróság szerint az egykori szegedi önkormányzati képviselő szerepet játszott egy 271 millió forintos költségvetési csalásban.

stories.title: A bundagyanúba keveredett elefántcsontparti csatárt beengedték Kanadába, játszhat a németek ellen
stories.summary: Szokatlanul sok fogadás érkezett arra, hogy Wahi sárga lapot kap egy francia bajnokin.

stories.title: Lannert Judit elárulta, honnan van a budai lakása és a balatoni nyaralója
stories.summary: Az oktatási miniszter havi keresete a vagyonnyilatkozata szerint az elmúlt három évben a 200-500 ezer forint közötti sávban volt.

stories.title: Most már eskü, tényleg megjelenik időben a GTA VI, jövő csütörtökön indul az előrendelés
stories.summary: A fejlesztő Rockstar Games egy rövid videóban jelentette be a dátumot, meg a hivatalos borítót is.

stories.title: Hegseth nekiment az európai NATO-tagoknak, felül fogja vizsgáltatni az amerikai katonai jelenlétet Európában
stories.summary: Az amerikai védelmi miniszter szerint a hozzájárulásukat is csökkentik a NATO költségvetéséhez, ha a tagállamok nem emelik a védelmi kiadásaikat.

stories.title: Talán nem túlzás állítani, hogy az angol szövetségi kapitányt kicsit zavarták a fotósok
stories.summary: „Egy kicsit elrontotta a mai nap élményét” – mondta róluk a meccs utáni sajtótájékoztatón, mert csapata bevonulásakor elállták előtte a kilátást.

stories.title: Miért tesznek a skót drukkerek közlekedési bójákat a szobrokra?
stories.summary: Magukkal vitték a hagyományukat Bostonba a jókedvű szurkolók.

stories.title: Hiába hivatkozott a Pázmány a katolikus szellemiség védelmére, jogerősen is pert nyert az oktató, akit egy LMBTQ-témájú írása után rúgtak ki
stories.summary: Másodfokon is Bognár Bulcsunak adott igazat a Fővárosi Ítélőtábla, az ítélet szerint az egyetem megpróbálta korlátozni, vagy ellehetetleníteni az LMBTQ-témájú kutatásokat.

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
stories.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

stories.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
stories.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

stories.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
stories.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

stories.title: Kazakhstan’s skies welcome Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup
stories.summary: Around 150 pilots from 33 countries have gathered in Kazakhstan's Zhetysu region for Central Asia's first-ever Paragliding World Cup.

stories.title: Iranian oil tankers pass US blockade ahead of peace deal signing ceremony
stories.summary: The naval blockade began on 13 April and targeted all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas.

stories.title: Washington's Lincoln Memorial pool turns green days after renovation
stories.summary: An algae bloom has turned Washington's newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool green just days after a $14 million makeover.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia home kills one, injures seven
stories.summary: At least one person was killed and seven others injured after a Russian drone struck a residential building in the southeastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia on 16 June, sparking a major fire and causing extensive damage, Ukrainian officials said.

stories.title: Javier Bardem joins Hollywood greats at landmark ceremony
stories.summary: Spanish film star Javier Bardem was honoured in Los Angeles on 16 June with a place in the celebrated forecourt of the TCL Chinese Theatre, a distinction reserved for some of cinema's biggest names.

stories.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
stories.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

stories.title: Thousands flock to Art Basel in Switzerland as market outlook improves
stories.summary: Art Basel 2026 in Basel draws thousands as high-end works by Picasso, Warhol and Hockney fuel a recovering global art market after years of decline.

stories.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
stories.summary: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".

stories.title: Norway's crown princess undergoes successful lung transplant, palace says
stories.summary: Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffered from pulmonary fibrosis, will now spend several weeks recovering in hospital.

stories.title: Hundreds of cats rescued from being eaten in Vietnam
stories.summary: More than 500 cats were seized in Vietnam after police dismantled a cat meat trafficking network, with dozens of animals later reunited with their owners.

stories.title: Messi hat-trick fuels Argentina's World Cup title hopes
stories.summary: Fans in Buenos Aires celebrated as Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick to lead defending champions Argentina to a 3-0 victory over Algeria in their World Cup opener.

stories.title: Iran pledges no nuclear weapons as US commits $300bn, leaked deal draft shows
stories.summary: The full US-Iran framework deal to end the war, as circulated during the G7 summit, would see Iran abandon its pursuit of a nuclear weapon in exchange for financial incentives to comply with the agreement.

stories.title: Trump jokes ‘I’m the boss’ as he arrives for G7 meeting
stories.summary: Donald Trump joked that he was “the boss” as he arrived for a G7 meeting in France on Wednesday. The light-hearted remark came before leaders gathered for talks on trade, economic challenges and global financial stability alongside representatives from partner nations.

stories.title: Bystanders smash windows of private jet to rescue passengers after crash
stories.summary: One of the six people on board died in the crash, a local official said. The five survivors were taken to hospital and are in a stable condition.

stories.title: Israeli settlers torch mosque in West Bank village of Jiljilya
stories.summary: Residents of Jiljilya, north of Ramallah, awoke on 17 June to find their mosque set alight overnight, with burnt tyres, Hebrew graffiti reading "revenge" and extensive damage left behind in an area of the occupied West Bank where Israelis are prohibited from entering.

stories.title: Netherlands hosts Japan’s emperor Naruhito to boost 400-year ties
stories.summary: The Netherlands welcomed Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako to Amsterdam on 17 June during an official visit aimed at celebrating the long-standing relationship between the two countries.

stories.title: Afghan government staff told to abandon smartphones or face prison sentence
stories.summary: How widely the rule is being implemented across Afghanistan and the scale of its impact were not entirely clear.

stories.title: Read the full text of the 14-point US-Iran agreement as released by Tehran
stories.summary: After a draft agreement was leaked earlier this week, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian published Tehran's copy of the framework deal he and US President Donald Trump separately signed on Wednesday. Here are its contents.

stories.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon ahead of anticipated US-Iran framework deal
stories.summary: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

stories.title: AI will create more jobs for humans, not replace them, Amazon founder Bezos says
stories.summary: The Amazon founder, who now has robotics and space travel companies, thinks AI will create a labour shortage.

stories.title: Trump says he will visit India as frosty relationship with Modi thaws
stories.summary: Trump made the announcement during chat where he also vowed to protect India.

stories.title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
stories.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

stories.title: Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life
stories.summary: In fiery exchange, judge sentenced him to three life sentences plus 25 years to life on four other charges, all running consecutively.
